title: Fans Speculate GTA 6 Trailer 3 Will Drop August 7 Ahead of Take-Two Earnings Call
summary: Players believe the third trailer could arrive on August 7 after Take-Two scheduled an unusual early-morning earnings call. Based on patterns from previous Rockstar titles, fans expect this trailer to focus on gameplay footage rather than cinematic sequences. The timing would align with Take-Two's summer marketing push commitment.

title: Analysts Predict Third Trailer Release Between Late July and Early August
summary: Industry experts analyzing Rockstar's historical marketing patterns suggest a new trailer is imminent. The window for Trailer 3 opens in mid-July based on GTA 5's release strategy, while Red Dead Redemption 2's timing points to late August for gameplay footage. Take-Two's August earnings call and global events provide strategic release opportunities.

title: Fans Await GTA 6 Update as Take-Two Schedules Unusual August 7 Financial Report
summary: Take-Two will release its quarterly report before market opening at unusual timing, sparking speculation about major announcements. Fans debate whether Rockstar will skip Trailer 3 entirely and move straight to gameplay footage. Four months before launch, zero seconds of gameplay have been shown publicly.

title: Rockstar UK Developers Form Union Amid Crunch Period, Potential Strike Threatens Launch
summary: Developers at five UK Rockstar studios are unionizing during the final development phase, seeking protections on crunch overtime and working conditions. A potential strike during this critical period could delay the November 19 release or result in launch bugs. This follows 31 employee terminations last year related to unionization efforts.

title: Rockstar Confirms No Physical Discs Planned for GTA 6 at Launch or After
summary: Hollywood Reporter sources confirm that GTA 6 will remain digital-only with no disc version planned for the foreseeable future. Customer support emails suggesting later disc availability were misinterpreted and referred only to code-in-box physical editions. This marks the biggest AAA title to fully abandon physical media.
